title: Gulf economies could be hit harder than during Covid
subtitle: Potential decline in non-oil GDP among GCC economies, %
source: Haver Analytics, Goldman Sachs GIR, March 2026
editorialPoint: For Oman, Bahrain, and Qatar, the economic hit from the Hormuz closure could exceed the damage from the Covid-19 pandemic — which was itself devastating
note: Beyond oil and gas, the war is negatively impacting tourism, aviation, logistics, hospitality, domestic trade, manufacturing, and financial services. GCC economies collectively lose ~$700M/day in oil revenue alone. Kuwait and Bahrain would lose a fifth of oil production over two months.
